dossiers.bear_case: Debt-funded pre-spin dividend to parent ($233M actual, vs $229M modeled, drawn from a freshly entered $1.0B secured credit facility) is the standard leveraged-spin pattern, though modest in absolute size relative to peers in this batch (HONA's $16B, MBGL's $2B). Company explicitly discloses its own pro forma omits real standalone dis-synergy and TSA costs, and the debt/dividend terms were still 'preliminary and subject to change' as filed -- already proven unreliable once actuals landed. Uncapped-style bidirectional indemnification with a two-year tax-driven restrictive covenant (prohibiting >40% ownership-change transactions) narrows strategic flexibility just as the company needs to prove itself standalone. No insider Form 4 data exists yet to corroborate or refute the conversion-only incentive structure.
dossiers.bull_case: Full-float, 1-for-1 spinoff (100% of shares distributed day one) with an explicit, if unquantified, index-mismatch selling risk baked into the filing -- the mechanical dump the Nemesis thesis wants. Equity award conversion preserves intrinsic value symmetrically for both Middleby- and SpinCo-side employees, a clean (if not conviction-signaling) alignment structure. Emerging Growth Company status and a brand-new, thinly-followed food-processing-equipment niche (Alkar) suggest real neglect once regular-way trading starts and the dump clears.
dossiers.forced_seller_map: 1-for-1 distribution ratio, 45,214,588 shares = 100% of SpinCo's outstanding shares distributed on day one (no lock-up-limited float). Explicit index-fund risk factor: 'Middleby stockholders who have an investment strategy of tracking an index fund may sell the shares of SpinCo common stock that they receive in the distribution if SpinCo is not listed on the same index.' No specific index name (S&P/Russell) disclosed, and no expected-float percentage quantified -- weaker specificity than some peers in this batch (e.g. MBGL quantifies 29% index-fund ownership), but the mechanism is present and explicit. MFP is a small food-processing-equipment carve-out (Alkar brand) from a mid-cap parent -- plausible small/mid-cap index or style mismatch, unconfirmed.
dossiers.key_risk: Actual pre-spin dividend/debt draw already diverged from the company's own modeled pro forma before the ink was dry, and real standalone dis-synergy costs are explicitly excluded from the earnings picture presented to incoming holders -- the true standalone P&L is unknown until the first post-spin 10-Q.
dossiers.post_spin_insider_activity: N/A -- pre-distribution as of this read. Regular-way MFP trading does not begin until 2026-07-07; only when-issued trading under MFPVV is underway. Zero Form 4s exist (confirmed via EDGAR submissions); only Form 3 initial-ownership statements have been filed for newly appointed officers/directors. Sweep deferred to a post-2026-07-07 run.
dossiers.pro_forma_notes: Company explicitly admits the pro forma excludes real standalone dis-synergy costs: 'such costs are not reflected in the unaudited pro forma condensed combined statement of earnings as these dis-synergies are not considered autonomous entity adjustments.' Same exclusion applies to TSA-related net costs. The $1.0B credit facility terms are stated as 'preliminary and subject to change' -- and the actual 6/29 8-K distribution ($233M, drawn from the full $1.0B facility plus cash on hand) already diverges from the modeled pro forma ($229M dividend funded by $252M of new debt), confirming the estimate was not reliable even in the near term. Share count/dilution effects are also explicitly disclaimed as unestimatable pre-spin.

dossiers.bear_case: $3.0B of new debt (Term A $1B + Term B $1B + Notes $1B) funded a $2.25B special cash payment to Comcast; underlying business is a structurally declining linear-TV/MVPD franchise (Q1 2026 revenue -1.1%, net income -22%, adjusted EBITDA -7% YoY), which the pro forma's steady-state framing does not capture. Dual-class structure with Roberts-family veto rights makes S&P 500 (re-)inclusion structurally unlikely -- there is no future index-buying catalyst to look forward to, unlike a normal single-class orphan. At 125 trading days post-spin (past the 90-day window), the mechanical dump this strategy is built to buy has very likely already cleared with no re-rating to show for it. No CEO/CFO open-market buying despite large paper grants.
dossiers.bull_case: Large, real forced-seller mechanism (S&P-500-excluded dual-class structure spun from a heavily passively-held parent) plus genuine outside-director cash buying ($6.19M from four independent directors including the new board chair) two months post-spin. First-ever dividend initiated and a $1B buyback authorized despite the leverage, signaling some management confidence in free cash flow generation from the declining-but-cash-generative cable/digital portfolio (GolfNow, Fandango, Rotten Tomatoes, SportsEngine growth assets alongside the legacy linear networks).
dossiers.forced_seller_map: 1-for-25 distribution ratio, ~144.5M shares issued day one, explicit index-fund forced-selling risk factor repeated in both the information statement and the 10-K. Unusually strong specificity here versus other names in this batch: VSNT retains a dual-class structure (Class A / Class B, Roberts family holding 33.33% non-dilutable combined voting power via Class B), and S&P has excluded multi-class share structures from S&P 500 eligibility since 2017 -- meaning the 'may not be included in the S&P 500' risk-factor language is closer to a structural certainty than a hedge. Comcast is a heavily passively-held S&P 500 constituent (Vanguard + BlackRock combined >20% of VSNT post-spin, both purely passive per 13G filings), so the forced-seller mechanism is real and large, but -- at 125 trading days post-spin, past the 90-day window -- whatever dump was coming has very likely already cleared without the stock meaningfully re-rating, and the dual-class exclusion means there is no future index-inclusion catalyst to look forward to.
dossiers.key_risk: The window has already closed (125 trading days, past the 90-day cutoff) with no re-rating and a structurally declining core business behind a dual-class share structure that forecloses future index-inclusion upside -- the thesis this strategy is built around does not apply cleanly here anymore.
dossiers.post_spin_insider_activity: Form 4 sweep since 2026-01-02: 4 insiders made 7 open-market purchases totaling $6,189,514 (Novak $5.27M, Hassell $360.7K, Conway $48.8K, Potter $510K, all in March 2026 at $36-38/share) -- a genuine, board-level vote of confidence with outside cash. But zero open-market purchases by CEO Lazarus or CFO Kini, whose $12.5M/$7.5M 'Founders Grants' are 100% award-conversion paper, not cash purchases. Officer buying is the signal that 'can't be engineered' per this strategy's own weighting; its absence here is a real gap even with the director-level buying as a partial offset. No sales found.
dossiers.pro_forma_notes: Unusually candid: management discloses standalone dis-synergies ($145M 9mo / $273M FY24) as a separate 'Management Adjustments' section outside the formal pro forma, cutting pro forma FY24 EPS ~19% ($7.23 to $5.83) -- a rare instance in this batch of a company volunteering the haircut rather than burying it. Formal pro forma also layers in $134-212M of new commercial/advertising SG&A and $10-45M of one-time systems costs. Actual Q1 2026 net debt ($2,952M) matched the pro forma estimate ($2,955M) almost exactly -- the capital-structure math held up, an honesty tell in that specific line. But revenue/profitability are independently declining (Q1 2026 revenue -1.1%, net income -22%, Adjusted EBITDA -7% YoY) on a structurally shrinking linear-TV/MVPD base -- a real business-quality concern the pro forma's steady-state framing does not surface.

dossiers.bear_case: The parent kept the growth and shipped the legacy: revenue +1% total/+3% organic in 2025, perpetual licenses -33%, US revenue down three straight years, Q1 2026 constant-currency revenue below prior year, substantial maintenance revenue on legacy products with a dedicated AI-disruption risk factor. Margins deteriorated into the spin (GAAP op margin 24%->21%, net income -20%); 2025 GAAP flattered by a $55M contingent-consideration reversal; $137M/yr capitalized software flatters FCF. Q2 2026 will report a ~$481M non-cash trademark impairment on top of first-ever interest expense (~$25M/yr) with only 8% goodwill headroom on $6.22B in a single reporting unit. Controlled company: MSAB 42.9% votes on 21.8% economics, no hard lockup on Rule 144 sales; 'independent' chair is a sitting Koch executive on Hexagon's nomination committee; two unremediated material weaknesses; unquantified IRS NOPAs (2019-2020) with Octave owning pre-spin taxes; uncapped indemnities to Hexagon; near-zero purchased insider equity (54,021 shares group-wide). US federal-gov revenue engineered to transition back to Hexagon over time. Sections: Risk Factors, 10-Q MD&A/Note 14/Item 4.
dossiers.bull_case: Textbook forced-seller setup nearly complete: confirmed OMXS30 and FTSE All-World deletions already traded; window trigger fired on both tells (vol_ratio 0.353, price stabilized, day 24 of 90). What the sellers dumped is a real business — $1.64B revenue, two-thirds-plus recurring, 97% gross retention, 30%+ adjusted margins, $320M FCF at ~1.3x leverage. Anchor aligned: MSAB kept its entire pro-rata 58.4M shares (21.8% economics, 42.9% votes, 100% of super-voting Class A) and filed a 13D; registered exit gated to May 2027. Management is the division's own operators (CEO ran Hexagon ALI 2017-2025) with pay restructured into OCTV equity: $850K base (cut from $1.03M) vs ~$8.75M RSU/PSU spin grant, majority PSUs on spinco-only metrics (rTSR/ARR/margin) through 2028. Orphan status (only Nasdaq Composite) is the neglect fuel; eventual Russell/S&P inclusion is the designated forced-buyer exit. Sections: Business/Key Metrics, CD&A, 13D, 8-K Item 2.03.
dossiers.forced_seller_map: DONE: OMXS30 tracker funds and FTSE All-World passives — both deletions confirmed traded (volume spikes 5.4M on 6/10, 3.4M on 6/11, 4.7M on 6/18 vs ~0.8M recent avg; 17.1% of S/O traded on the US line in 24 sessions; -38% peak-to-trough to the 6/22 low $15.65, then stabilization). IN PROGRESS: benchmark-constrained Swedish/European active mandates (Swedbank Robur 6.7% of Class B is the archetype), softened because the Stockholm SDR line (~78% of shares initially) means holders are index-forced, not venue-forced; free SDR->US conversion ends ~Nov 2026, concentrating migration into H2 2026. MAY SELL LATER: MSCI/STOXX trackers if carried pending reclassification (UNCONFIRMED — residual risk); residual SDR holders force-sold at program termination ~2028; MSAB's 58.4M shares — constrained until May 2027 by RRA gating plus Rule 144 affiliate volume limits, with structural disincentive to sell Class A (dilutes 42.9% voting control); 13D Item 4 reserves the right to sell. MUST BUY: today only Nasdaq Composite trackers; Russell adds Sept/Dec 2026 if spin-off rules qualify (unverified), else June 2027 recon; S&P committee-dependent.
dossiers.key_risk: The forced-seller map may be incomplete: MSCI/STOXX treatment is unconfirmed, Stockholm SDR-line turnover is invisible to the US-line volume trigger that declared the window open, and the SDR free-conversion window ending ~Nov 2026 creates a slow-burn residual supply pipeline. If a residual European index deletion or SDR-driven supply wave lands on top of the pre-announced Q2 charge stack ($481M trademark impairment, first interest expense, material weaknesses), the name takes a second leg down with no confirmed forced buyer until Russell Sept/Dec 2026 at the earliest. DISSENT ON RECORD (2026-07-02 launch re-read, operator-instructed): an independent single-session read of the same documents scored incentive 0.40 (below the own-gate) / garbage 0.50 / verdict watch — the own verdict is gate-marginal and contested; the deep read stands on process depth (9-reader extraction + adversarially-refuted scores), not on certainty. If OCTV grades badly, read the dissent first.
dossiers.post_spin_insider_activity: Form 4 sweep 2026-07-03 since distribution (2026-05-29): zero filings — structural, not behavioral: Octave is a foreign private issuer (Irish plc) and FPIs are Section 16-exempt, so insiders never file Form 4s. The channel is dark for this name; incentive_alignment rests on the Form 10 comp structure and the MSAB 13D, and the score already discounts for the missing visibility ('FPI status weakens insider-trade visibility' — rationale).
dossiers.pro_forma_notes: $1.64B revenue, 66-72% recurring, GRR 97% (99% large customers), NRR 105%, ARR $1,117M +8.7%, SaaS +25% in Q1 2026, adjusted operating margin 30-31%, FCF ~$320M. Debt: term loan $350M + EUR150M fully drawn plus ~$120M + EUR25M revolver draws (~$660M total) funding the $625M payment to Hexagon — ~1.3x adjusted operating income, ~2.0x GAAP operating income, ~1x covenant-basis EBITDA vs 3.50x covenant, ~$355M undrawn revolver. No DB pension (10-Q Note 8 DC-only), no material litigation (Note 11), no going-concern language. Thin cash: $80M pro forma plus $57M HQ sale-leaseback (10-Q Note 14).

dossiers.bear_case: New $150M CD&R-held Series A preferred stock carries a 7% cash/PIK dividend escalating to 10% on a Triggering Event, and can BLOCK ALL COMMON DIVIDENDS while a Triggering Event is occurring -- a real, structurally senior claim on the business that functions similarly to a dumped liability even though it's equity-labeled. The Tax Matters Agreement creates a poison-pill-like overhang: any acquisition of ADI shares that breaks tax-free treatment makes ADI responsible for the resulting tax liability, discouraging the very M&A/re-rating catalyst an orphan spinoff would otherwise hope for. $900M dividend plus $67M cash payment to Resideo, funded by $1B of new debt at a blended rate materially above the historical allocated cost. No insider or ownership data exists yet to offset any of this.
dossiers.bull_case: Legacy Honeywell environmental indemnification (from Resideo's own 2018 spin) was fully resolved and paid by Resideo pre-spin via a $1.59B settlement -- ADI inherits none of that overhang, a genuine positive versus a name that might otherwise carry legacy-parent liability. Governance is reasonably clean (double-trigger change-in-control vesting, no single-trigger acceleration at spin). Pro forma candor on near-term unprofitability is a positive honesty signal relative to peers.
dossiers.forced_seller_map: 1-for-2 distribution ratio, ~75.4M shares. CD&R Channel Holdings II retains 19.69% (a real, disclosed sponsor overhang, though CD&R also holds the new preferred so its incentives are aligned with the capital structure, not necessarily with common holders). BlackRock 13.02%, Dimensional 5.21%, Vanguard 5.00% -- ~23.2% combined passive/quant ownership received mechanically regardless of index fit. Circumstantial evidence (Resideo's PSU awards reference the S&P SmallCap 600 as a TSR benchmark) suggests Resideo sits in that index; ADI's own prospective index membership is not disclosed either way.
dossiers.key_risk: The new senior preferred stock's escalating-rate, dividend-blocking terms are a structurally embedded claim ahead of common equity that functions like a dumped liability, compounded by a tax-driven poison-pill provision that discourages the M&A catalyst this strategy's orphans often need to re-rate.
dossiers.post_spin_insider_activity: N/A -- pre-distribution (Distribution Date August 3, 2026, still about a month out). No Form 4 filer relationship exists yet under this CIK. Sweep deferred to a post-distribution run.
dossiers.pro_forma_notes: Unusually candid on near-term unprofitability: pro forma Q1 2026 net loss available to common stockholders of $(6)M, EPS $(0.08) -- rare for a spinoff information statement to show a per-share loss rather than a dressed-up profit. But the $51M FY2025 / $8M Q1-2026 dis-synergy estimates are managements' own soft estimates likely to be revised upward once TSA services are actually replaced, and the pro forma interest expense (~$68-71M on the new $1B debt at ~6.78%) is materially higher than the $44M historically allocated -- a real, disclosed leverage step-up easy for a reader to skip past.

dossiers.bear_case: This is a leveraged extraction executed at the bottom: $4.3B of new debt raised expressly to pay FedEx a ~$4.1B cash dividend (8-K 6/1/26 Item 8.01; HoldCo Note 3, F-10), leaving $250M cash, $4,264M debt, and $(562)M pro forma equity. Standalone earnings power is far below the headline history — $388M/yr of FedEx cash-pool interest income vanishes, ~$209M/yr interest expense begins, +$63M/yr permanent incremental self-insurance cost, and 9M pro forma net income is $156M vs $497M historical (-69%, pro forma note b/m). The latest quarter was an operating LOSS of $(6)M and core operating income roughly halved even excluding $126M separation costs (F-41); management concedes demand is declining through FY26 and Amazon is entering LTL. The spinco self-funds ~$1.0B of repurchased receivables carrying a $181M allowance (~15% of gross), bears an uncapped tax indemnity to FedEx, and rents its own brand (5+5-year license that dies on change of control), which — stacked with the 2-year TMA 50%-ownership-change prohibition, the notes' >50% acquisition restriction, and a 5-year classified board — forecloses the takeover exit. Insiders own <1% combined (393,441 shares, all converted FDX holdings; five directors own zero), have bought nothing in the open market through day ~32, and collected ~$1.1M cash plus ~$2.5M of RSUs merely for completing the spin, while legacy LTI plans were amended to credit target performance for all post-FY26 periods.
dossiers.bull_case: Highest-quality asset Nemesis has seen in a barge wrapper: the largest North American LTL carrier ($8.9B FY25 revenue, 84.2% operating ratio, top-5 customers only 7% of revenue, ~355 terminals — Info Statement 'Our Business') spun at a visible cyclical trough (average daily shipments 99.7K FY23 -> 85.9K 9M FY26; Q3 FY26 operating loss includes $126M one-off separation costs). Even the trough year generated $1,531M operating cash flow against $437M capex (F-18), servicing 4.77% IG-laddered debt with no maturity until 2029. The supply calendar is defined and non-fundamental: FedEx's 19.9% (29.75M shares) must clear within 24 months of 2026-06-01, likely first via price-insensitive creditor debt-for-equity exchanges, on top of index flowback the filing itself warns about — exactly the discount mechanism that lets a scaled LTL franchise trade at a gap to ODFL/SAIA/XPO comps. Management is now paid on standalone adjusted FCF/EPS through 2028 (75% PSU LTIP, CEO 450% of salary target, granted 2026-06-29 per the 6/26 8-K), and CEO John A. Smith — the operator credited with 860bps of Freight margin expansion since 2018 — severed all parent roles. Overhang clearance plus any freight-cycle inflection rerates it; vol ratio 0.64 and falling says flowback is already being absorbed.
dossiers.forced_seller_map: MUST SELL: (1) FedEx — 19.9% retained stake (29,752,544 of 149,505,248 shares) must be disposed within 24 months of 2026-06-01 (deadline ~June 2028) to preserve Section 355 tax-free status; disposal machinery pre-built via the Stockholder and Registration Rights Agreement (registration on demand, explicit 'will not limit or prohibit any such sale or transfer', no lockup), voting neutralized by proportional proxy so it is pure supply. FedEx stated the stake exists to be monetized for its own deleveraging ('Reasons for FedEx's Retention'). Likely first tranche: debt-for-equity exchanges with FedEx creditors — banks receiving stock they immediately distribute/sell, a price-insensitive block event. (2) Index/mandate flowback — pro-rata 1:2 distribution to the entire FDX register; risk factors explicitly warn index funds will sell if FDXF 'is not a participant in the index'; BlackRock 5.20%, Dodge & Cox 5.45%, PRIMECAP 4.01% are mechanical conversions of FDX positions, classic mandate-mismatch sellers. Index treatment of FDXF is not disclosed in any filing read. (3) Minor: Computershare fractional-share sales at distribution (done); insider vest-date supply begins May 15, 2027 (Spin-Off RSUs + first LTIP RSU third). MUST/CAN BUY: eventual index inclusion of a ~$149.5M-share NYSE large-cap (undisclosed, plausible); LTL pure-play investors comping vs ODFL/SAIA/XPO. NO takeover bid can absorb supply: 2-year TMA prohibition on 50%+ ownership change, notes indenture >50% acquisition restriction, classified board to the fifth annual meeting, trademark license terminates on change of control. WHEN: flowback now (day ~22/90, vol ratio 0.64 falling); creditor exchange blocks anytime inside 24 months; window likely opens within weeks.
dossiers.key_risk: The trough is not the trough: a deepening freight recession compresses EBITDA toward the leverage covenant (3.75x stepping to 3.50x seven months post-spin per HoldCo Note 3; ex-separation-cost annualized EBITDA of roughly $1.3B against $4.26B debt already implies ~3.3x) just as the 29.75M-share FedEx disposal lands, with negative book equity, no dividend support, no insider buying to signal a floor, and an uncapped tax indemnity as tail risk.
dossiers.post_spin_insider_activity: Form 4 activity since distribution (2026-06-01): no open-market purchases; no sales.
dossiers.pro_forma_notes: Pro forma at 2/28/26: cash $250M, total debt $4,264M ($3.7B notes 4.30-5.25% due 2029/31/33/36, WA 4.77%, first maturity 2029; $600M 3-yr term loan fully drawn 5/27/26; $1.2B revolver undrawn), stockholders' DEFICIT $(562)M. Earnings bridge is the crux: FY25 net income $1,346M historical -> $899M pro forma ($6.06 EPS); 9M FY26 $497M -> $156M ($1.05 EPS). Drivers: -$388M/yr related-party cash-pool interest income (artifact of FedEx sweep, gone at spin), +$209M/yr interest expense, +$63M/yr incremental standalone self-insurance, $287M separation costs already on the 9M FY26 P&L, ~$10M/yr transitional costs. Pro formas are honest — they fully surface the standalone downgrade. Favorable splits: FedEx retains substantially all legacy self-insurance reserves ($393M retained-earnings benefit, pro forma note m) and occurrence-based pre-spin insurance liabilities; pension transfer is active-U.S.-employees only with an estimated net ASSET (note f) but 'may differ materially' and imports Q4 MTM volatility (+$96M in FY25 PF). Watch items: ~$1.0B receivables repurchased after factoring termination with $181M allowance (~15% of gross) and 9M provision tripling to $46M; leverage covenant 3.75x -> 3.50x at month ~8 vs ~3.3x implied on ex-cost trough EBITDA; TSA cost parity with historical allocations is management assertion 'subject to change' against $628M/yr of allocated shared services deemed 'not practicable to estimate' standalone. Reader contradiction (reader 3 pro forma note m says FedEx keeps most self-insurance reserves; readers 4/6 show $438M accruals on spinco books and EY's sole critical audit matter) resolved conservatively: treated as materially traveling with the spinco until the final Employee Matters Agreement split is quantified in the first standalone 10-Q.

dossiers.bear_case: No incentive edge and decelerating fundamentals into the listing. Form 4 sweep: zero open-market insider purchases in the four months since the NYSE debut (31 post-listing Form 4s, all award-conversion mechanics); the CFO owns zero shares; all 14 officers/directors together hold ~0.14% (Form 10 Item 4). Management's existing PSUs were converted to time-vesting at a deemed 85.5% of maximum vs 40.6% actual achievement in the most recent completed cycle, with holding periods and RSU underpins dropped at completion (8-K 2026-03-02 Item 5.02) - alignment was reduced, not increased, at the listing moment. Earnings are rolling over: 9M FY26 net income -10.2%, Q3 adjusted EBITDA margin 41% vs 44%, Gen Tool dollar utilization down four straight periods (53%->47%), UK segment near breakeven ($7M Q3 adjusted operating profit) and under a ~$42M restructuring; FY25 hurricane revenue ($90-100M) does not recur. FCF strength is a capex-cut artifact ($2,054M->$1,440M rental capex) that management's own countercyclical framing labels late-cycle. $549M of 1.500% notes mature Aug 2026 and roll at ~5.5-5.95% coupons against $39M of cash. First US reporting cycle opens with a declared material weakness (debt classification) and a restatement. And the flowback discount Nemesis would harvest may never appear: the LSE secondary listing lets UK holders keep the line, UK CGT rollover removes tax-forced selling, and the company's $1.5B bid absorbs what remains.
dossiers.bull_case: World-class franchise mispriced by flow, not fundamentals: #2 North American equipment renter (~11% share, Form 10 Item 1), $10.8B FY25 revenue, adjusted leverage 1.6x (Item 2 MD&A) with the only financial covenant a springing 1.0x FCCR that has never been tested ($3,230-3,431M ABL availability vs $475M trigger; 10-Q Note 9). Pension risk extinguished via March 2024 bulk-annuity buy-in covering the entire DB membership (funded status -$1M; Form 10 Retirement Benefit Plan Obligations). A $1.5B buyback deliberately commenced March 2, 2026 - the first NYSE trading day - and runs to April 2027 (10-Q Note 15), a standing corporate bid under the stock through the entire FTSE-deletion flowback window, on top of $1,047M repurchased in the prior nine months. Countercyclical FCF ($1,428M in 9M FY26) funds the progressive dividend plus buyback. As a Delaware-incorporated, NYSE-primary, profitable US filer, SUNB is a plausible S&P index-inclusion candidate once seasoned - the filing itself frames 'potential inclusion in U.S. equity indices' as a transaction rationale (Item 1A) - and inclusion would force index buying against an already-shrunken float. Mega-project pipeline ($840B 2023-25 to $1.3T+ 2026-28, Item 1) cushions weak local construction.
dossiers.forced_seller_map: SELLERS (mostly already done): FTSE 100/All-Share trackers were mechanically ejected when Ashtead's LSE primary listing was cancelled - Ashtead ceased LSE trading before the 2026-03-02 open and SUNB holds only an 'International Commercial Companies Secondary Listings' category admission (Form 10 MD&A conditions (a)-(d); 8-K 2026-03-02) - so index deletion selling concentrated around late Feb/early Mar 2026, now ~4 months past. The pre-conversion register was Dodge & Cox 11.6% (active, may stay), BlackRock 8.2% and Vanguard 5.2% (index sleeves = structural sellers of the FTSE portion) per Item 4. Slow-tail sellers: UK income funds facing 30% US dividend withholding absent treaty paperwork, UK corporates whose Part 9A dividend exemption gets no withholding credit, and DI holders exposed to UK IHT situs risk (Item 11). One-time mechanical supply: ESOT sold all 359,482 shares ($25M) on the scheme date (10-Q Note 15). DAMPENERS: 1:1 scheme with UK CGT rollover (no tax-forced disposal), no stamp duty on DIs, and the retained LSE line under the same SUNB symbol. BUYERS: the company - $1.5B repurchase program live 2026-03-02 through April 2027, after $1,047M bought in 9M FY26; prospectively S&P/Russell index funds on US inclusion (timing undisclosed; three passive 13Gs filed April 2026 show US institutional onboarding underway). NET: the window (day ~87/90) closes with the acute flow event already cleared and the residual tail met by a standing corporate bid - the classic Nemesis entry never materialized as a dislocation.
dossiers.key_risk: Category error: this is the whole Ashtead group in a Delaware wrapper, not an orphaned spinco, so the playbook's rerating engine (US index inclusion) has undisclosed timing - the Form 10 itself lists 'delay in our inclusion in North American indices' as a stock-price risk - while fundamentals (utilization, net income, margins) are visibly decelerating and the Aug-2026 $549M refi reprices interest cost ~4x on that tranche. If deceleration outruns the index catalyst, the stock derates on earnings before any flow-driven rerate arrives; conversely the $1.5B corporate bid means the flowback dislocation a watcher waits to buy may never get cheap.
dossiers.post_spin_insider_activity: Form 4 activity since distribution (2026-03-02): no open-market purchases; no sales.
dossiers.pro_forma_notes: There are no pro-formas to distrust - the Form 10 presents full Ashtead Group consolidated actuals (US GAAP-converted), and the FY26 10-K (filed 2026-06-23) contains the audited year. Balance sheet as inherited: total debt $7,644M at 2026-01-31 ($6.2B across nine senior note series at 1.5%-5.95% plus $1,489M drawn on the $4.75B ABL secured by substantially all assets), 81% fixed rate, vs $39M cash - liquidity deliberately run through the ABL. Adjusted leverage 1.6x; sole covenant is a springing 1.0x FCCR inactive above $475M availability (availability $3,230M, never tested). Near-term: $549M 1.500% notes due 2026-08-12, callable at par from May 2026, refi at prevailing 5.5-5.95% adds roughly $22-24M annual interest on that tranche. Off-balance-sheet adjacent: $2,876M lease liabilities, $2,402M net DTL (accelerated fleet depreciation - a cash-tax drag if fleet growth stalls), $159M discounted self-insurance provisions, contingent consideration up to $35M. Pension: fully bought-in (bulk annuity, March 2024), funded status -$1M. Quality caveats: material weakness in debt current/non-current classification forced restatement of the H1 FY26 interims (CEO/CFO declared disclosure controls not effective as of 2026-01-31; remediation began Feb 2026, unproven); Form 10 award-fair-value disclosure error ($44.41->$60.97) corrected in the 10-Q; auditor churn (Deloitte resigned 2023, PwC UK -> PwC US, Deloitte re-engaged for PCAOB re-audit of FY23); $66M of 9M restructuring/relisting costs added back to adjusted figures. SOX 404 first required for FY ending 2027-04-30.

dossiers.bear_case: The equity holder pays for Honeywell's deleveraging: $15.1B of the $16.0B note proceeds went to the parent ($9.1B cash + $6.0B Exchange Notes; Description of Material Indebtedness, Interim Note 9), leaving pro forma equity NEGATIVE $(3,454)M (Capitalization). Earnings quality is flattered: $328M/yr non-cash pension income, non-GAAP segment profit strips recurring environmental remediation ($389M in 2025), stock comp, and the trademark fee (Interim Note 16); net income declined three straight years into the spin ($2,886M→$2,687M) while sales grew 26%, and Q1 2026 GAAP operating cash flow was -$225M with working capital propped by factoring ($193M Q1) and $474-521M supply-chain financing (Note 2). Parent rent is real: $1.125B trademark toll over <5 years for a name Honeywell can terminate at year 6 or on unconsented change of control; environmental tail across ~327 sites accrued at the LOW end of ranges with the excess 'not possible' to estimate, after a $181M methodology step-up booked on Honeywell's watch (Note 18); uncapped tax indemnity plus 2-year TMA bans on buybacks/major issuance/CoC remove both capital-return support and the takeover bid. Insider skin is thin (<1% group, zero open-market buys) and legacy PSUs converted with performance 'deemed achieved.' The stock is already +12% since day one — the spin discount this strategy hunts has largely been paid away.
dossiers.bull_case: Crown-jewel franchise, not a discarded division: $17.4B FY2025 sales (+12.7% YoY), 25% adjusted EBIT margin, $18.6B backlog, systems on ~90% of the in-service fleet, no platform >8% of revenue (Info Statement Summary; Note 5/19). Despite the $16B note stack it launches investment-grade (S&P BBB+ positive / Fitch A- / Moody's A3, MD&A Credit Ratings), with a benign ladder ($0 due rest of 2026, $1,257M 2027-28, $13B post-2030, mostly fixed at 4.95% WAvg), no financial maintenance covenants, $4B undrawn revolver + $4B CP backstop, and ~$1B cash. It receives an OVERFUNDED U.S. pension as a $2,727M net asset (pro forma note (c)). FY2025 FCF $3,260M covers $839M interest ~4x. Real management commitment signals: Jepsen left the sitting Deere CFO seat and Donofrio left the JCI GC seat to join; go-forward pay is denominated in HONA equity (CEO $13M target LTI vs $4M at HON). Dividend 'in line with peers' retains income holders; likely index inclusion (undisclosed, but 316.9M-share large-cap) forces mechanical buying. Separation costs, TSA drag and the $225M/yr trademark toll all roll off within ~5 years, so standalone prints get cleaner each quarter.
dossiers.forced_seller_map: Who must sell: (1) distribution agent sells aggregated fractional shares in the open market in the first ~7-10 days (mechanical, tiny; 8-K Item 1.01); (2) ESG/defense-constrained funds — 41% Defense & Space revenue, and the risk factors explicitly flag ESG holders 'precluded' from the name; (3) style-mismatch HON holders — the Board itself listed post-spin selling by 'shareowners whose investment objectives may not be met' as a negative factor (Reasons for the Separation) — but the planned peer-line dividend blunts income-fund ejection. Who must NOT sell: insiders are irrelevant to float (<1% combined) and directors are barred from selling below 5x retainer. No parent overhang: Honeywell retains ZERO shares (8-K Item 5.01). Who must buy: index funds if HONA enters S&P 500/large-cap indices — treatment undisclosed in the filings (listed only as a stock-price risk factor), but a ~317M-share IG large-cap makes inclusion probable; Vanguard (7.51%) and BlackRock (6.68%, stale Jan-2024 13G) carry over as passive anchors. Who cannot buy: acquirers — Section 355(e) presumption plus uncapped indemnity plus classified board to 2030 sterilizes control demand for ~2 years. Timing verdict: day ~5, price +12% — flowback is being absorbed by natural buyers already; the forced-seller mispricing this trade needs has not appeared, and the 6-month budget is for watching whether ESG/index rebalancing (Aug-Sep index reviews, first standalone print ~Aug 2026) creates a late dislocation.
dossiers.key_risk: Balance-sheet asymmetry against open-ended legacy liabilities: with $(3.45)B negative book equity and $16B of notes, any adverse revision of the environmental tail (327 sites, low-end-of-range accruals, $181M step-up in the final pre-spin year, excess loss explicitly unquantifiable — Note 18 was a Critical Audit Matter) or a draw on the UNCAPPED tax indemnity to Honeywell (TMA: not 'limited in amount or subject to any cap') lands on an equity account with zero book cushion, while the +12% post-spin run means the market is already pricing clean standalone execution.
dossiers.post_spin_insider_activity: Form 4 activity since distribution (2026-06-26): no open-market purchases; no sales.
dossiers.pro_forma_notes: Pro forma as of 3/28/2026: cash $1,000M, total assets $21,363M, liabilities $24,817M, total DEFICIT equity $(3,454)M on ~316.9M shares; capitalization was 'not yet finalized' at this amendment (Capitalization fn 1). FY2025 pro forma: sales $17,404M, net income $1,780M (vs $2,687M historical — a 34% haircut from $839M interest at 4.95% WAvg on $16B notes), EPS $5.62, or $5.45 after $68M of recurring 'management adjustments' the pro formas EXCLUDE. Honest-pro-forma check: historical P&L carries essentially zero interest (Note 1: parent debt never attributed), $328M/yr of pro forma earnings is non-cash pension income on the conveyed $2,727M net pension asset, $225M/yr trademark fee sits in SG&A but is stripped from every non-GAAP measure, and segment-profit margins are computed on Flexjet-adjusted sales — so headline 'Adjusted EBIT $4,406M' materially overstates GAAP cash economics. Gross leverage ~3.6x pro forma Adjusted EBIT; total scheduled debt service $29,577M (MD&A). One-offs largely behind: Flexjet $434M cash paid (Dec 2025 + Q1 2026), but separation costs run through FY2027 (incl. ~$100M 2026 separation capex) and $93M of 2026 internal-reorganization tax costs. Contradictions resolved conservatively: distribution effective 12:01 a.m. June 29, 2026 per the completion 8-K (not June 26 as the task context states); U.S. pension shown as multiemployer (off balance sheet) in historicals but conveyed as a $2,727M net asset in pro formas — both readings are correct at their respective dates, and I treat the pension primarily as an earnings-quality flattering item rather than pure balance-sheet credit.

dossiers.bear_case: Textbook leveraged spin for the parent's benefit: S&P Global's board — not MBGL management — sized $2.0B of notes and extracted ~$1.9B of the proceeds (The Separation — Incurrence of Debt; Reasons for the Separation), cutting FY25 pro forma net income 37% to $139M ($0.47 EPS) and leaving tangible equity deeply negative (goodwill $8,845M + intangibles $3,714M ≈ 95% of $13,162M assets). Insider skin-in-the-game is near zero: directors and executive officers own <0.05% combined, four directors own zero shares, all legacy SPGI PSUs convert to TIME-vesting MBGL RSUs (performance hurdles extinguished; year-of-spin at target), only ~$76M of employee equity company-wide converts to MBGL, the post-spin comp program is "not yet finalized," and a 14% share pool with liberal recycling awaits (CD&A; Security Ownership). Zero Form 4 buys through day 3. Operations are softening into the print: Q1-26 net income -4.7% on +8.1% revenue, B2B segment profit collapsed to $5M from $14M, FCF down to $48M from $62M, tax rate 32.5%, and pro formas exclude standalone public-company costs as "not quantifiable" — so the first earnings report may disappoint rather than rescue. Structure is hostile to rescue paths: uncapped indemnities to SPGI (even for consented actions), two-year TMA blocking combinations and >20% buybacks, SPGI's sitting CFO on the board deemed "independent," CEO's brother paid $500K in contingent transition payments, and a dividend commitment layered on $113M/yr interest.
dossiers.bull_case: Forced-seller technical on a genuinely high-quality asset: MBGL is CARFAX (FY25 segment revenue $1,142M, segment op profit $322M and rising) plus B2B auto data — 81-84% subscription revenue, 41% Adjusted EBITDA margin ($711M FY25), $461M FCF against only $24M capex (info statement MD&A; Summary Combined Financial Data). The filing itself maps the seller: ~29% of SPGI is held by index funds (Risk Factors, as of 5/15/26) that "will likely be required to sell" if MBGL is not added to the S&P 500, "where there may not be offsetting demand" — and the dump is observably in progress (-9% from day-one close at day 3). Balance sheet, while parent-serving, is survivable: $2.0B notes laddered 2029/2031/2036 at blended 5.74%, ~2.5x net leverage vs a 3.50x covenant, $500M undrawn revolver, ~$200M opening cash; EBITDA/interest ~6.3x. Confirmed clean of classic barge cargo: no defined-benefit pension (401(k) only, Note 5), no material litigation (Business — Legal Proceedings). Management is operator-lifer, not parent transplant: CEO Eager 20+ years at CARFAX, Fredericks 28 years, LaFeir a Polk veteran; directors take $400K 3-yr-cliff RSUs. Demand catalysts inside the patience budget: first standalone print (~Aug 2026), sell-side initiations, possible S&P/Russell index placements, and an intended quarterly dividend. If insiders start buying on Form 4 (channel is demonstrably active — 11 Form 3s filed), the missing alignment leg appears and this converts to own.
dossiers.forced_seller_map: SELLERS (now through ~day 60): (1) Index funds holding ~29% of SPGI (Risk Factors, 5/15/26) — S&P 500 trackers must sell MBGL absent index inclusion; the issuer's own launch press release (8-K Ex-99.1, 7/2/26) repeats that "significant amounts... will likely need to be sold in the open market where there may not be offsetting demand." No inclusion decision is disclosed in any filing read. (2) Active large-cap quality/growth SPGI holders with mandate mismatch — the board itself flagged "sales by S&P Global's stockholders whose investment objectives may not be met" (Reasons for the Separation). (3) Computershare's mechanical open-market sale of aggregated fractional shares in the first days (Item 2.01). Supply is unimpeded: all ~295.1M shares distributed 1:1 on 7/1/26, no lockup, affiliates hold <0.05%, SPGI retained 0%. Observed: -9% from day-one close by day 3 — the map is executing. BUYERS: nobody is forced. BlackRock (8.21%) and Vanguard (7.59%) inherit positions but their retention tracks index assignment. Potential demand arrives with S&P MidCap/500 committee placement (timing undisclosed), Russell treatment at reconstitution, sell-side initiations and the first standalone print (~Aug 2026), and dividend-mandate buyers once the payout is declared. Company self-help is capped: TMA limits buybacks to <20% aggregate for two years and blocks stock-funded M&A, so the tape must clear on outside demand. WATCH TRIGGER: Form 4 channel is active (11 Form 3s on 6/4 and 7/1); cluster insider buying into this dump is the signal that converts watch to own.
dossiers.key_risk: The catalyst that should end the flowback may extend it: pro formas explicitly omit incremental standalone public-company costs ("not quantifiable at this time," pro forma preamble) while booking only +$12M of autonomous adjustments — yet stand-up costs already ran $21M in Q1-26 alone and B2B segment profit fell to $5M. If the first standalone quarter (~Aug 2026) prints below the $0.47 PF EPS run-rate with $113M fixed interest plus a dividend commitment, forced index selling hands off to fundamental selling, and the 2-year Tax Matters Agreement (no takeover, buybacks <20%, waiver only in SPGI's sole discretion) removes both rescue bids and aggressive repurchase support.
dossiers.post_spin_insider_activity: No Form 4 transactions filed since distribution (2026-06-30) as of the sweep.
dossiers.pro_forma_notes: FY25 pro forma (10-12B/A Ex-99.1, Unaudited Pro Forma): revenue $1,750M, operating profit $327M, interest expense $113M (blended 5.74% incl. fee/OID amortization), net income $139M, EPS $0.47 on 295.1M shares — vs $220M historical net income (interest never existed historically: $13M FY25, all intercompany). Q1-26 PF: NI $35M, EPS $0.12. Balance sheet at 3/31/26 PF: cash $200M, LTD $1,981M net, equity $9,927M (down from $11,583M after the $1.9B distribution to SPGI); goodwill $8,845M + intangibles $3,714M ≈ 95% of $13,162M assets; DTL $983M; intangible amortization $296M/yr. Honesty assessment: interest math is straightforward, but pro formas book only +$12M/yr autonomous costs ($3M TSA incremental + $9M new rent) and explicitly EXCLUDE standalone public-company costs as "not quantifiable" — while actual stand-up costs ran $21M FY25 + $21M in Q1-26 alone, and carve-out corporate allocations were $112M FY25 with the company "unable to quantify" standalone equivalents. Treat PF earnings as a ceiling. Other cash frictions: assumed tax payments to parent $196M FY25 vs $26M actual cash taxes (standalone cash taxes step up); cash sweeps not remitted at separation; $74M signed-not-commenced leases; Canada Carfax Loan ($227M to SPGI sub) — Readers 4/6 differ on its fate, resolved via pro forma note (g): settled/eliminated at separation ($236M related-party settlement to APIC), so it does not survive. Leverage: ~2.8x gross / ~2.5x net vs 3.50x covenant (4.00x acquisition step-up); no maturity before 2029; 101% special mandatory redemption moot post-completion.

dossiers.bear_case: Pure pre-revenue cash burner — the parent kept ALL recurring royalty revenue (Jemperli/GSK, imsidolimab/Vanda; 10-Q MD&A). Pro-forma ~$171M annual net loss vs ~$174.8M cash (Q1 burn $32.6M) implies only ~12-18 months runway despite "two-year" marketing, so a dilutive raise is near-certain — and it would land into a live 10,497,054-share (~28%) PIPE resale overhang unlocked by the 5/15 424B3 (no lock-up). Largest anchor EcoR1 already sold $65M at spin (Form 4); there are zero post-spin open-market insider buys. Lead asset rosnilimab is being shopped ("ROE-maximizing strategic options") rather than self-advanced, and its UC Phase 2 failed. Officers are shared with the parent on "majority" time, and EGC/SRC status suppresses comp disclosure. China CMO/Section 232 supply exposure.
dossiers.bull_case: Clean, well-capitalized inverted spinoff: TRAX holds the operating pipeline (rosnilimab, ANB033, ANB101) and launched with ~$174.8M cash ($100M from AnaptysBio + ~$80M PIPE), debt-free, with the parent absorbing nearly all liabilities (10-Q Note 9/Note 5). Informed money set a $13.81 institutional PIPE clearing price; the stock now trades ~$19.30, above PIPE. Management equity is genuinely spinco-metric: AnaptysBio $75/$100 PSUs were cancelled and re-anchored to the TRAX price and the new CFO got a fresh $2.55M TRAX grant (4/20 8-K Items 1.01/5.02). TRAX qualified on the 4/30 Russell rank day (~$800M cap) and was added at the 6/26 reconstitution, giving it index-fund holders. Rosnilimab posted positive Phase 2b in RA and carries partnership/out-license optionality.
dossiers.forced_seller_map: SELLERS (mostly behind us): the distribution was TAXABLE/dividend-treated, so tax-driven flowback plus AnaptysBio-holder mandate mismatch largely played out at/after the 4/20 spin; EcoR1 already monetized 4,705,575 shares ($65M) via the pre-arranged PIPE secondary and retains only 9.1% (13G); the full 10,497,054-share PIPE block (~28%) became freely resaleable via the 5/15 424B3 — overhang now LIVE, no disclosed lock-up. BUYERS: Russell index funds were forced in at the 6/26/2026 reconstitution effective close (rank-day 4/30 qualified), explaining the 6/18–6/30 volume/price bumps — a one-time mild support. NET: forced selling is largely done and index buying already passed; there is no clean forward forced-buyer imbalance to trade today.
dossiers.key_risk: Financing/dilution: ~$171M pro-forma annual net loss against ~$174.8M launch cash (Q1 operating burn $32.6M, 10-Q) leaves only ~12-18 months of runway; the company must raise, and doing so into the already-live 10.5M-share PIPE resale overhang (5/15 424B3) is the dominant near-term risk. REFUTER CORRECTION (2026-07-03): the draft's incentive 0.5 rested on misreading converted parent PSUs as spinco-anchored equity; corrected to 0.30 — verdict unaffected (watch either way).
dossiers.post_spin_insider_activity: Form 4 activity since distribution (2026-04-06): no open-market purchases; 1 insider(s) sold $64,999,990 across 1 transaction(s).
dossiers.pro_forma_notes: Balance sheet is exceptionally clean: no debt, no pension/OPEB, no transferred litigation (ordinary-course only). Parent absorbed AP ($3.1M), accrued ($25.8M), leasehold improvements, and even the operating-lease liability — 10-Q Note 5 shows a non-cash transfer of $(14,112)K lease liabilities and $12,886K ROU to AnaptysBio — so TRAX occupies HQ via a short pass-through sublease (6/22 8-K), a continuity dependency (terminates if parent's Master Lease ends) rather than a balance-sheet liability. Section 336(e) election gives a FMV stepped-up tax basis. Honesty ding: "two-year runway" marketing vs pro-forma ~$171M loss on ~$174.8M cash is really ~1yr at modeled burn — resolved conservatively to the tighter figure. Residual contingents: assumed employment liability for ALL current AND FORMER AnaptysBio employees (4/20 8-K), reverse-TSA org burden, unquantified parent indemnities, Centessa $10M off-balance-sheet milestone. Volume check: recent ~0.33-0.88M shares/day sits at/below the ~0.79M week-1 average and near the 673K 30-day avg — no 254x event exists in the OHLCV.
